feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: where to generate the SSH key pair for SFTP adapter in EIC with private key authentication
entry.content: I am deploying an iFlow on EIC with both source and target systems using SFTP adapters with private key authentication. I want to confirm whether the key pair should be generated on EIC or CPI, and what configuration steps are required on EIC and CPI. I need guidance on the proper setup process for private key authentication in this scenario.

entry.title: EDI Integration Templates for SAP Integration Advisor have mode configure-only
entry.content:
Hi!!!

We need to use the following template in Discover (Integrations) in Integration Suite:

The EDI integration templates for SAP Integration Advisor are read-only, and we cannot change anything.

Thank you

entry.title: Building an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot: A Practical Approach to Intelligent SAP Support
entry.content:
Every SAP implementation eventually reaches the same stage Production Support. As organizations grow, support teams receive hundreds of requests every month. Many of these requests are repetitive and are not caused by system defects, but by business process misunderstandings, authorization issues, workflow delays, master data validations, or integration failures.
Typical examples include:

  • Unable to create a Purchase Order
  • Invoice posting failures
  • Sales Orders blocked
  • Workflow approvals pending
  • User authorization issues
  • SAP Integration Suite interface failures
  • Background job failures

Although these issues are common, users still need to create support tickets and wait for the support team to investigate.

This made me think differently.

What if every SAP user had an intelligent assistant that could understand the business process, analyze the issue, and provide the right guidance before a support ticket was ever created.

This idea led to the design of an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot.

The objective was simple:

  • Reduce repetitive support tickets.
  • Provide immediate guidance to business users.
  • Improve user experience.
  • Allow SAP support teams to focus on complex business problems rather than repetitive operational issues.

Solution Overview:
The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot is designed on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
Instead of acting as a simple chatbot, it combines enterprise knowledge, live SAP data, business process understanding, and AI reasoning.
When a business user asks a question, the Copilot:

  • Understands the SAP process.
  • Retrieves enterprise knowledge.
  • Validates live SAP information.
  • Determines the probable root cause.
  • Suggests the appropriate resolution.
  • Automatically escalates the issue when technical intervention is required.

This creates a much faster and more intelligent support experience.

Architecture Overview:How the Solution Works

Step 1 – User Raises a Question
Instead of creating a support ticket, the business user simply asks the AI Copilot.

Example: Why can’t I create Purchase Order 4XXXXXXXXX?
The Copilot immediately begins analyzing the request. 

Step 2 – Understanding the Business Context
The first responsibility of the Copilot is to understand what the user is asking.
It identifies:

  • SAP Module
  • Business Process
  • Business Object
  • User Context

In this example, it identifies:

Module: Procurement
Business Object: Purchase Order

Step 3 – Searching Enterprise Knowledge

The Copilot searches enterprise knowledge that has been prepared by the SAP project team.
This knowledge includes:

  • Business Process Documents
  • Functional Specifications
  • Standard Operating Procedures
  • Configuration Documents
  • SAP Notes
  • Previous Incident Resolutions
  • Integration Documentation
  • Support Runbooks
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Unlike traditional chatbots, the Copilot searches only trusted enterprise knowledge.

Step 4 – Validating Live SAP Data
The next step is validating the issue against the SAP system.
Using SAP APIs, the Copilot performs live validations.

For a Purchase Order issue, it checks:

  • Vendor Status
  • Purchasing Organization
  • Material Availability
  • User Authorization
  • Approval Workflow
  • Master Data Validation
  • Related Integration Status

This allows the AI to understand the actual business situation rather than simply searching documentation.

Step 5 – AI Reasoning
After collecting information from SAP and the enterprise knowledge repository, the AI determines the root cause.For example, suppose the Vendor has been blocked.

Instead of showing a technical SAP error message, the Copilot explains:

Vendor 1XXXX is blocked for Purchasing Organization 1XXX. This is a business restriction rather than a system issue. Please submit a Vendor Activation Request using the Procurement workflow.

The user immediately understands:

  • Why the issue occurred.
  • Who owns the issue.
  • What action needs to be taken.

No support ticket is required.

 Why This Solution Is Different:

Traditional chatbots simply search documents. The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot goes much further.

It combines:

  • Enterprise Knowledge
  • SAP Business Processes
  • Live SAP Data
  • AI Reasoning
  • Operational Knowledge

This enables the Copilot to provide meaningful business guidance rather than generic answers.

Business Benefits:

The solution can provide significant operational benefits.

  • Faster resolution of common SAP issues.
  • Reduced repetitive support tickets.
  • Improved first-time resolution.
  • Better employee experience.
  • Consistent troubleshooting across SAP modules.
  • Reduced investigation effort for SAP support teams.
  • Better utilization of SAP functional and technical consultants.
  • Enterprise knowledge becomes available whenever it is needed. 

Conclusion:

Artificial Intelligence should not replace SAP consultants.Instead, it should make enterprise knowledge available to every business user at the moment they need it.The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot demonstrates how SAP Business Technology Platform, enterprise knowledge, and AI can work together to transform traditional support into an intelligent digital experience.

Rather than waiting for answers, users receive immediate guidance.SAP support teams can focus on innovation and business transformation.As organizations continue their SAP modernization journey, combining AI with enterprise business knowledge presents a practical opportunity to improve user experience, increase operational efficiency, and build a smarter support organization.

entry.title: pick file from AL11 through CPI and send to external SFTP server
entry.content: pick file from AL11 through CPI and send to external SFTP server

entry.title: FTP ISSUE FOR BIDDEN
entry.content: while connectivity FTP, getting a forbidden issue

entry.title: Request for information for SAP Intgeration Suite
entry.content:
Hi, we are using SAP Integration Suite to devlopp mutilple Flow and we have some questions :

Concurrency & Threading

 

What is the maximum number of concurrent iFlow executions (worker threads) allowed per tenant on the Integration Suite Starter/Edge plan? Is this configurable or fixed?

When multiple instances of the same iFlow run in parallel, are they queued, rejected, or executed with degraded performance? What is the exact queuing/throttling behavior?

Does concurrent execution of the same iFlow share any runtime resources (JVM heap, thread pool, Camel routes) that could cause performance degradation as concurrency increases?

 

HTTP/Message Processing Timeouts

 

What is the maximum synchronous HTTP request processing timeout enforced by the Integration Suite runtime (Cloud Foundry router + CPI runtime) for a single message exchange? Is this configurable at the iFlow, tenant, or platform level?

Is there a difference in timeout behavior between the HTTPS Sender adapter and other trigger types (e.g., timer-based, JMS-based)?

If a message exceeds the timeout mid-processing, is there a way to receive a partial-comp

entry.title: Testing SAP standard API on RISE backend
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When testing SAP Standard OData APIs on SAP RISE, who in your team owns and maintains the Postman collection?

Who should provide the following? 

 1. OAuth 2.0 setup for IAS

 2. Environment variables for Dev/QA/Prod

 3. Example requests for all CRUD operations

It’s working well, but before we roll this out company-wide I’d like feedback:

1. Is there an SAP recommended way to package + version Postman collections?

2. Any security concerns sharing collections with tokens in them?

3. Does SAP plan to publish official Postman collections for all S/4HANA Cloud APIs?

4. Could you please guide me to setup postman collections?

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: I cannot add a GitHub repository in SAP Integration Suite despite having Tenant Admin privileges.
entry.content: I am trying to set up GitHub for Integration Suite. My account has Tenant Admin privileges, but the option to add a repository is disabled. Also, I noticed there is no EDIT option in the repository details table, as described in the SAP Documentation. I need help resolving this issue.

entry.title: GitHub Integration Access and Configuration for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I would like to request information regarding the GitHub integration feature available in SAP Integration Suite.

In the Integration Suite interface, I can see Git Push and Git Pull options. However, I do not have the necessary configuration options or documentation to use this feature.

I am looking for guidance on the following:

  1. How do I connect SAP Integration Suite to a GitHub repository?

  2. How do I authenticate or log in to GitHub from my SAP Integration Suite tenant?

  3. Where can I find or configure the Git repository URL for my Integration Suite environment?

  4. Are there any roles, authorizations, or tenant configurations that must be enabled before Git integration becomes available?

  5. Are there any prerequisites on the SAP BTP or Integration Suite side to enable this functionality?

  6. Is this feature available with our current SAP Integration Suite Enterprise Standard Edition subscription, or does it require an additional

entry.title: 【Integration Suite】VlaueMappingのレコード投入をIFlowで自動化したい
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite（Cloud Integration）のValue Mappingへのデータ投入を自動化したい。
iFlowで下記処理を自動するイメージです。

１，sftp連携でcsvファイルを取込
２、csv⇒xmlに変換
３、メッセージマッピングでValueMappingの構造にレコードを設定
４、ValueMappingに既に登録されているレコードを全件削除
５、メッセージマッピングのレコードをValueMappingに投入

上記処理の内、４、５のやり方が不明です。
教えていただけますでしょうか。

よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Optional permitted values in custom tags are not working anymore
entry.content: In der Cloud Integration sind unter Custom Tags neuerdings keine Optionalen Werte mehr möglich. Die aktuelle Version verlang zwingend einen Wert in dem Feld "Permitted Values". Somit sind keine Freitexte mehr für die Entwickler möglich.

entry.title: Dynamic query parameter not working in SAP CPI HTTP adapter (OAuth 2.0 API)
entry.content:
. Generate Identifier using POST API

2. Generate Key using GET API with Identifier

3. Fetch data using GET API with Key

4. iFlow Details

Package Name: NAF_FICO Integration for S4 HANA

Integration Flow Name: BLOOM_BERG_DATA

5. Issue Description:

 

Step1: POST call successfully generates Identifier.

Step2: Identifier is stored and passed dynamically via Content Modifier as query parameter.

GET call does not return Key and gives empty response.

Same Identifier works correctly in Postman.

* Please find the attached screenshots in step 6

entry.title: ACL for Products in SAP Integration Suite Developer Hub
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Is it possible to define ACLs (Access Control Lists) for products in the Developer Hub? I have already read your guide regarding roles and role collections for the Developer Hub, but I could not find any information on how to set permissions specifically for products.

Could you please advise if this is supported, and if so, point me to the relevant documentation or steps?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Success factors unable to perform link breaks
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

When posting the data from SAP CPI to success factors EPM forms using SF V2 adapter, we see that payload that needs to be posted to that from has line break added. Where as when we see it in SF EPM form every thing is getting added in a single line.

We used line breaks like br tags, - in the groovy scripts to create post data in SF EPM with line breaks. But is is not working, can you please check and provide us the solution.

Thanks

Pradeep

 

Thanks

Pradeep

entry.title: Issue deploying the iflow in Integration suite
entry.content:
We have developed an integration and on deploying it is throwing an error - Error Details[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][InstanceError] : {"message":"CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED"} 

We have checked all the configuration and can confirm all looks fine. 

Package - Delta Load from IBP - POC

Iflow - test_delta_load_for_manual_page_count 

Please check and let us know the rot cause and resolution.

entry.title: MySQL connectivity from SAP Integration Suite - workaround needed
entry.content:
Hello,

We're trying to set up a connection from SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration), via the JDBC adapter (with Cloud Connector), to an on-premise MySQL database

In the JDBC Data Source configuration, there's no separate type for MySQL, only MariaDB, SQL Server, Oracle, and DB2.

Our research indicates that on-premise MySQL connectivity via the JDBC adapter is not supported.
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003533889

Does a workaround exist for this, or can you give us advice?

Kind regards, Jurgen

entry.title: Edge Integration Cell with API-centric integration on Developer Hub
entry.content:
Hi, with recent rolling our API-centric integration and Integration Cell. I look everywhere in the document and don't see any option to publish APIs (develop with new API artifact, and deployed on Edge Integration Cell) on Developer Hub. I'd like to know if it's available now or in our roadmap. Please let me know. Thanks.

If you said no, please provide an SAP document link or a note for that.

entry.title: Not able to see APIM in integration suite
entry.content: We have already added APIM management capability to Integration suite. Assigned all the Admin access to the user. But we are not able to see API Management in Integration Suite.

entry.title: Services available in China region for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello All,

If one of you have worked for projects based out of China, can you clarify what Integration services are available from SAP Integration Suite? From SAP notes, I see not all services of SAP Integration Suite are completely available in China region. 

Regards,

Naresh

entry.title: File-based S/4HANA migration tool: Interoperability Review or Integration Certification?
entry.content:
I'm building an ISV solution and want to understand which certification track applies before I lock the architecture.

What it does: migrates financial data (balance sheet, P&L, WIP) from SAP R/3 to S/4HANA — reads a customer's own extract (e.g. CJI3), applies a customer-specific mapping, and generates journal-entry load files plus a validation report evidencing a zero delta.

  • Runs as a stateless multi-tenant SaaS on AWS, not on SAP BTP.
  • Read-only towards the source — no ABAP transport, no code in the customer landscape.
  • Today it produces files; it does not call SAP APIs directly.

It has already been used on a real enterprise migration; I'm now bringing it to market as a product.

Questions:

  1. Under the Jan-2026 framework, would a file-based tool like this fall under Interoperability Review rather than Integration Certification?
  2. If we switched the output to the released Journal Entry – Bulk Create API, would that on its own qualify us for Integration Certification — or does that track also require running on SAP BTP?

Pointers to documentation I may have missed are just as welcome as an answer.
